topics.label: The Forge
topics.recapText: I already told you about the forge. Three generations of honest steel.

topics.label: Rumors
topics.recapText: I already told you about the bandits on the north road. Nasty business.

greeting.speakerText: Aye, what brings you to my forge? I've got blades that need sharpening and orders backing up.

return_greeting.speakerText: Back again, are you? Well, I'm still here. What do you need?

repair_greeting.speakerText: Repairs? Aye, I can fix up most blades. Bring me the weapon and 50 gold.

responses.displayText: Surely you can do better for a fellow adventurer?

repair_haggle.speakerText: I've got to eat too, friend. 50 gold is fair for the work. Take it or leave it.

responses.displayText: What if I bring you materials too?

repair_persuade_pass.speakerText: Ha! You've got a silver tongue. Fine — 30 gold, but don't tell anyone I gave you a deal.

repair_persuade_fail.speakerText: Save your honeyed words, friend. 50 gold or nothing. That's my final offer.

repair_accepted_dialogue.speakerText: Good. I'll have it ready by morning. Come back tomorrow.

rumors_greeting.speakerText: Rumors? Well, I don't usually gossip, but... I've heard there's trouble on the north road. Bandits set up camp near the old watchtower. Merchants are afraid to travel.

responses.displayText: Tell me more about these bandits.

rumors_bandits_detail.speakerText: A dozen at least, from what the traders say. Led by some scarred woman with a two-handed axe. They're not just robbing folk — they're organized.

rumors_watchtower_detail.speakerText: Half a day's ride north, past the split in the road. You'll see it on the hill. Used to be a guard post before the garrison pulled out.

bandit_camp_response.speakerText: The bandit camp? Aye, I've heard the same as everyone else. Bad business on the north road. If you're thinking of doing something about it, you're braver than most.

forge_greeting.speakerText: This forge has been in my family three generations. My grandfather built the original bellows — still works, too. We make honest steel here, nothing fancy, but it'll keep you alive.

flattery_pass.speakerText: Ha! Flattery will get you everywhere, friend. Tell you what — I've got a piece I've been working on. Not for sale normally, but for someone with taste... let me show you.

flattery_fail.speakerText: I appreciate the kind words, but I've heard better from traveling merchants trying to lowball me. You want something, speak plain.
